fault_hypothesis: On line 72, `int(x)` truncates fractional allocations, causing the sum of allocations to be less than `total` when weights don't divide evenly. The fix should use proper rounding (e.g., largest-remainder method) instead of simple truncation.

spec.expr: sum([int(x) for x in planned__1]) == int(total)

constraints.intent: After truncating planned values to int, their sum must still equal total — this fails when fractional parts are lost.
After the integer allocation list is built, its sum must equal the total to detect truncation loss.
Before returning, the final allocations must sum to total regardless of which branch was taken.
At the end of the integer-allocation branch, the sum of truncated values must equal total.
The returned allocations must always sum to total.
